learning_outcomes.description: Analyse how the employment relationship is contractually structured, including the legal thresholds for incorporating workplace policies and implied terms into express contracts.
Evaluate how worker status is determined across jurisdictions, contrasting Australia’s text-based binary model with the UK's three-tier framework and New Zealand’s reality-based test.
Assess how employers use cascading restraints of trade, non-compete clauses, and extraterritorial provisions to protect commercial interests and manage cross-border employees.
Formulate strategic legal arguments for adverse action and general protections claims within the statutory architecture and reverse onus frameworks of the Fair Work Commission.
Navigate workplace disputes including considering an employee's protected right to raise complaints, interpersonal misconduct, bullying and sexual harassment.

Critically evaluate how modern regulatory frameworks govern systemic workplace harm, focusing on the mandatory positive duty, psychosocial hazards, workplace surveillance, the right to disconnect and work from home arrangements.
Advise on the legal and commercial complexities of workplace termination, including genuine redundancy, business transfers or collapse, and unfair deactivation for regulated platform workers.
Reflect critically on the advocacy strategies, jurisdictional hurdles, and tactical performance demonstrated within a simulated cross-border dispute.

assessments.task_description:
There will be 4 mini quizzes based on the allocated readings and the material covered in lectures. Each quiz will be online, and will be multiple choice, true/false and the like.

The mini quizzes collectively contribute 10% of the final grade. Students are required to complete at least three of the four quizzes to be eligible for the full 10% available for this assessment task. Each completed quiz is worth 3.33% of the final grade. Where a student completes all four quizzes, only their best three quiz results will be counted towards the final mark for this assessment. 

The mini quiz must be submitted electronically during class. You must be in the class seminar to undertake this mini-quiz.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
The assessment will be released on Monday 7 Sept 2026 at 9.00am.

You will examine the application of comparative employment law in relation to a current issue. Further details will be provided on Blackboard. The word limit will be 2000 words. The word limit should be detailed on the first page of the assessment. Footnotes are not included in the word limit and a bibliography is not required. Any words over the word limit will not be marked.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The assignment must be submitted electronically via the assessment submission link on the course Learn.UQ (Blackboard) site.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Alternative Dispute Resolution Individual Presentation – Cross-border scenario
assessments.task_description:
• The task will involve a Conciliation Advocacy Simulation involving a cross-border scenario.
  • There will be allocated individual 20 minute blocks to deliver submissions as a Senior Lawyer in a live online session. These submissions will be delivered one on one to your lecturer.
  • Full assessment details will be released on this assessment on Blackboard along with randomly allocated individual session times on 12 October 2026.

assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
• After your Assessment Task 3 presentation, you will submit an individual reflection of no longer than 1000 words reflecting on Task 3. The submission requires students to critically self-evaluate their strategic performance during the statutory conciliation advocacy presentation.
  • Full assessment details will be released on this assessment on Blackboard on 12 October 2026.

learning_activities.topic:
Day 1 - The Doctrinal Foundations & The Contractualisation of Work

• Topic 1: Historical evolution and overview of Australian employment law and international influences.

• Topic 2: Distinguishing employment contracts from services contracts

• Topic 3: Textual construction of express terms; linguistic thresholds for policy incorporation; implied terms.

• Topic 4: Comparative worker status (AU text binary vs. UK three-tier framework vs. NZ reality-based s 6 ERA test).






Learning outcomes: L01, L02
learning_activities.topic_title: Day 1 - The Doctrinal Foundations & The Contractualisation of Work
learning_activities.topic_description: Day 1 - The Doctrinal Foundations & The Contractualisation of Work • Topic 1: Historical evolution and overview of Australian employment law and international influences. • Topic 2: Distinguishing employment contracts from services contracts • Topic 3: Textual construction of express terms; linguistic thresholds for policy incorporation; implied terms. • Topic 4: Comparative worker status (AU text binary vs. UK three-tier framework vs. NZ reality-based s 6 ERA test).

learning_activities.topic:
Day 2 - Restraints of Trade, Cross-Border Employees: Extraterritoriality, General Protections & ADR in the FWC

• Topic 5: Restraints of trade, reasonableness, cascading clauses, and the "blue pencil" rule.

• Topic 6: Managing cross-border employees, extraterritorial provisions, and corporate hub friction.

• Topic 7: General Protections - Statutory architecture of adverse action, protected workplace rights, the reverse onus of proof, anti-discrimination provisions; Alternative dispute resolution in FWC.


Learning outcomes: L03, L04

learning_activities.topic:
Day 3 - Bullying, Sexual Harassment, WHS (positive duties and psychosocial hazards), Remedial Damages and Termination – Pt 1

• Topic 8: Bullying and Sexual Harassment under the Fair Work Act 2009 (Cth)

• Topic 9: Positive Duties and Management of Psychosocial Hazards

• Topic 10: Termination – Pt 1: Lawful Dismissal and Unfair Dismissal


Learning outcomes: L05, L06, L07

learning_activities.topic:
Day 4 - Termination – Pt 2, Right to Disconnect, Workplace Surveillance, & WFH Arrangements

• Topic 11: Termination – Pt 2 (Genuine redundancy; Redundancy payments; Transfer of business; Business collapse; Unfair deactivation for regulated workers)

• Topic 12: The right to disconnect, "reasonable refusal" of contact, workplace surveillance and work from home arrangements


Learning outcomes: L06, L07
